records.stage_evidence: Report describes findings from a KUB X-ray, not an IVP procedure

ocr.verbatim:
X-RAY -KUB
Bilateral psoas shadows are normal
• B/L Renal shadows are normal .
Single large with multiple small radio opaque shadows seen in right renal region with single radio opacity seen left renal region
The bowel show gases and faecal masses and some soft calculus can be missed
No other radiological abnormality is seen
OPINION : - 1-- FINDINGS ARE SUGGESTIVE OF B/L RENAL CALCULUS
ADV- IVP
(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg no 16250
(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg No 1721
REPORT CHECKED BY ROHIT BMRIT
NOT FOR MEDICO LEGAL PURPOSE

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a KUB X-ray, not an IVP. It describes normal psoas and renal shadows, but notes radio-opaque shadows in both renal regions suggestive of bilateral renal calculi. The report was checked by Rohit BMRIT.
key_observations: Report is for a KUB X-ray, not an IVP
Findings suggest bilateral renal calculi
Normal psoas and renal shadows noted
Report checked by Rohit BMRIT

records.stage_evidence: Diagnostic IVP images showing contrast in renal pelvis and ureters; no catheter or stent visible
records.summary: A set of IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) radiographs showing the pelvicalyceal system with contrast material. Multiple views (PA, lateral, oblique) are displayed, with measurements of 14.94 mm and 14.6 mm noted on two images. The patient is a 29-year-old female.
key_observations: Multiple IVP images (PA, lateral, oblique views) of the abdomen and pelvis
Contrast material visualized in renal pelvis and ureters
Measurements of 14.94 mm and 14.6 mm noted on two images
Patient identifier: 29/Y/27/36/F/I V P,Chest/27-Mar-2026

records.summary: Four ultrasound images of the abdominal renal region, showing cross-sectional views of kidneys with measurements of renal parenchymal thickness and depth.
key_observations: Four separate ultrasound images of the kidneys.
Measurements of renal parenchymal thickness and depth are annotated on each image.
Images show varying kidney sizes and parenchymal echogenicity.
Ultrasound machine settings (CS-1, 32Hz, TISO.3, MI 1.2) are visible on each image.

ocr.verbatim:
ULTRASOUND WHOLE ABDOMEN
NAME 
SEX / F 
R/BY 
DATE- 27 March 2026
LIVER Size Normal Echotexture homogenous IHBR normal Portal vein normal
GALL BLADDER Size normal Pericholecystic collection absent Wall thickness 4 mm Lumen echo free
C B D Caliber Normal Course smooth
PANCREAS -- Size normal Echotexture normal, homogenous Pancreatic duct normal
UPPER RETEROPERITONEUM ===
Para aortic lymphadenopathy absent, Aorta and IVC normal
RT. KIDNEY-- Size normal, Corticomediullary differentiation is maintained, there is Grade I hydronephrosis with PUJ calculus of size 13.2mm seen. Renal parenchymal thickness is normal. Multiple calculi of various sizes largest measuring approx. 8.5mm seen in right kidney.
LT KIDNEY -- Size is normal, Corticomediullary differentiation is maintained, there is Grade I hydronephrosis with PUJ calculus of size 14mm seen. Renal parenchymal thickness is normal.
SPLEEN -- Size normal, Echotexture is normal Splenic vein normal with absent splenic collaterals.
FREE FLUID - Absent, at time of examination.
URINARY BLADDER - Wall thickness 3-4 mm, Content - Echo free.
TAS-
UTERUS - Size is normal 7.5x4.6cms, retro verted/retro flexed homogenous echotexture. Endometrial thickness normal. no fluid seen in cul-de-sac
B/L Adnexa are clear.
OPINION
1. B/L Grade I hydronephrosis with PUJ calculus. (ADV-XRAY KUB)
2. Right renal calculi.
DR. 
Radiologist
UKMC reg.no 16250
Checked by-----Muskan-----if any typing error please contact mob -8433003384.
NOT FOR MEDICO LEGAL PURPOSE

records.summary: This is a typed ultrasound report of the whole abdomen, dated March 27, 2026. It details findings on the liver, gallbladder, pancreas, kidneys, spleen, bladder, and uterus. Key findings include bilateral Grade I hydronephrosis with PUJ calculi (13.2mm on right, 14mm on left) and multiple calculi in the right kidney. The report also notes no free fluid, normal spleen, and normal uterine and adnexal findings.
key_observations: Findings from a whole abdomen ultrasound performed on March 27, 2026.
Bilateral Grade I hydronephrosis with PUJ calculi (13.2mm right, 14mm left).
Multiple calculi in the right kidney, largest measuring 8.5mm.
Normal liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, and bladder.
Normal uterus and adnexa with no fluid in cul-de-sac.
Report signed by a radiologist with UKMC registration number 16250.
Note: 'NOT FOR MEDICO LEGAL PURPOSE' at bottom.

records.stage_evidence: Report describes post-void film and final opinion after contrast study

ocr.verbatim:
Patient Name: 
Ref. By: 
Part Scanned IVP

INTRA VENOUS PYELOGRAPHY

KUB 
Bilateral psoas shadows are normal
B/L renal shadows are normal
Multiple radio opaque shadows seen in right renal with single opacity
left renal s/o calculus

IVP done with 80 c.c of contrast (Double Contrast) and film were taken at
5 min, 15 min, 45min with pre and post voiding.

15 -45 min. FILM * 
Right side nephrogram & pylogram seen. normally with normal right
ureter seen

45---Min film 
Left side nephrogram & pylogram seen. normally with normal left
ureter Up to lower end

FULL BLADDER
Normal in size, shape and contour.
No calculus seen.
No diverticula are seen.
Post voiding film shows no significant amount of urine.

OPINION:- 1- FINDINGS ARE SUGGESTIVE OF B/L NORMAL EXCERETORY KIDNEY
2-- MULTIPLE CALCULUS LARGEST OF 14 MM SEENIN RIGHT KIDNEY .
3-- LEFT PUJ CALCULUS OF 14 MM SEEN

M.D
(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg no 16250

M.D
(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg No 1721

NOT FOR MEDICO LEGAL PURPOSE

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an Intravenous Pyelography (IVP) performed on a 29-year-old female. The report details findings from multiple films taken at different time intervals after contrast injection, including KUB, 15-45 min, 45-min, and post-void films. The opinion section notes normal excretory kidneys bilaterally, multiple calculi in the right kidney (largest 14 mm), and a calculus in the left PUJ (14 mm).
key_observations: Patient is 29Y/F, IVP performed with 80 c.c. double contrast
Right kidney shows multiple radio-opaque shadows with a single opacity
Left kidney shows calculus (s/o calculus)
Right and left ureters appear normal on respective nephrograms/pyelograms
Full bladder is normal in size, shape, and contour
Post-void film shows no significant urine
Opinion: Bilateral normal excretory kidneys, multiple calculi in right kidney (largest 14 mm), left PUJ calculus (14 mm)
Report signed by two radiologists with UKMC registration numbers

records.summary: Abdominal X-ray showing a ureteral catheter in the pelvis, likely post-procedure. The image includes the spine, pelvis, and surrounding soft tissues.
key_observations: A ureteral catheter is visible extending from the upper abdomen into the pelvis.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visible.
Image header indicates patient age 29, female, date 04-Apr-2026, and facility ROORKEI.
